description_long: This is a heavily armored, tracked combat vehicle with a distinctive rhomboid shape reminiscent of early 20th-century tanks, such as the British Mark I. The exterior is covered in thick, riveted metal plating, with visible hatches, access panels, and protruding weapon sponsons on each side. The tracks run the full length of the vehicle, wrapping around the body for maximum traction and mobility over rough terrain. The surface is uniformly rust-brown, suggesting a weathered steel construction, and there are no visible windows or glass, emphasizing its protective design. The vehicle features side-mounted gun barrels and various mechanical details, such as hinges and bolts, that reinforce its utilitarian, military purpose. No visible markings, insignia, or camouflage patterns are present, and the vehicle lacks traditional wheels, relying entirely on its continuous tracks for movement.
description: A rust-brown, heavily armored tracked combat vehicle with a rhomboid shape, side-mounted gun barrels, and full-length tracks. The exterior is rugged, riveted steel with no visible markings or camouflage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the tracks, a prominent side sponson with a gun barrel, and various access panels and hatches.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the sloped armor, central track sections, and a protruding gun barrel on the left side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with a similar sponson and gun barrel, as well as visible surface details and panels.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the tracks, a rear hatch, and the rearward extension of the side-mounted gun barrel.
attribute_weapon_mounts: Two visible side-mounted sponson gun barrels, one on each side of the vehicle.
attribute_armor_plating: Full body coverage with thick, riveted steel armor plating; sloped sides and reinforced panels are visible.
